claims.text: By late May 2026 the shares had at one point risen nearly 1,600% since listing, lifting the market value above HK$880B (about US$112B).
Peak market cap, ~$112B, as of 28 May 2026.

On 8 January 2026 Zhipu listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange (ticker 2513) at HK$116.20 a share, raising about HK$4.35B (US$560M) and closing its first day at HK$131.5, up 13.17%, for a market capitalization near HK$57.89B.
Lofty valuations reflect a scarcity premium and limited tradable shares due to lock-up periods.
That is roughly a thousand times revenue.
FY2025 revenue grew 131.9% to ¥724M (≈$105M), but the statutory net loss widened to ¥4.72B (the adjusted loss was ~¥3.2B), with R&D alone running about ¥3.18B — roughly 439% of revenue.

In H1 2025, about 84.8% of revenue came from on-premise/private deployment — high-touch, project-based systems work for Chinese government and state-owned buyers — while the cloud API that is MaaS contributed only 15.2%.
blended gross margin fell from 56.3% in 2024 to 41.0% in 2025 as lower-margin cloud and delivery-heavy deployment grew, and the cloud-API line itself ran a negative gross margin in H1 2025.
Cumulative losses from 2022 through H1 2025 totalled roughly ¥6.2B.
cutting GLM API prices to as low as 0.1 yuan (~$0.014) per million-token prompt — about 90% below the industry average.
customer concentration is falling (top-five customers reportedly dropped from ~55% to ~40% of revenue).
On 16 January 2025 the US Bureau of Industry and Security added ten Zhipu-affiliated entities to the Entity List (90 FR 4617).
contrary to some reporting, the Zhipu entries carry no "Footnote 4" or military-end-user designation — they reference only Section 744.11 of the EAR.

The legal effect is a license requirement for all items subject to the EAR with a presumption of denial — the broadest standard entry, foreclosing legal access to Nvidia's leading-edge accelerators and US software.

These additions are being made because these entities advance the People's Republic of China's military modernization through the development and integration of advanced artificial intelligence research.
Zhipu's GLM-Image was, per the company and Chinese state media, trained end-to-end on Huawei Ascend Atlas 800T A2 hardware with the MindSpore framework and no US chips.
GLM-5 then completed full-stack adaptation across seven domestic chip platforms — Huawei Ascend, Moore Threads, Hygon, Cambricon, Kunlunxin, Muxi and Enflame.

Independent analysis finds Huawei's flagship CloudMatrix 384 (Ascend 910C) matches Nvidia GB200-class system performance only by drawing about 4.1x the power and at 2.5x worse power-per-FLOP, and that some Chinese labs struggled to complete training runs on Ascend even with Huawei engineers on site.

GLM-4.5 (July 2025) was the discontinuity — Zhipu's first Mixture-of-Experts model at 355B total / 32B active parameters, trained on 23T tokens and released under the permissive MIT license, alongside a compact GLM-4.5-Air at 106B/12B.
Zhipu's own GLM-4.5 technical report put the model 3rd of 14 on its averaged benchmark suite, with 91.0% on AIME 24 and 64.2% on SWE-bench Verified.
GLM-4.6 (October 2025) pushed context from 128K to 200K tokens and claimed over 30% better token efficiency.
on Zhipu's own GLM-4.6 grid, SWE-bench Verified came in at 68.0% versus Claude Sonnet 4.5's 77.2%.
By mid-2026 the family had reached GLM-5.2: a 744B-total / 40B-active MoE with a one-million-token context window, still MIT-licensed.

Artificial Analysis rated the June-2026 GLM-5.2 the leading open-weights model on its Intelligence Index (51), placing it fourth overall — behind Fable 5, Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5, but ahead of DeepSeek V4 Pro and MiniMax-M3.
on the human-voted LMArena leaderboard GLM-4.6 sits clearly below the frontier Western cluster.
GLM-5.2 lists at roughly $1.40/$4.40 per million input/output tokens, about one-sixth to one-seventh the cost of GPT-5.5 or Claude Opus.
Chinese models reached ~61% of OpenRouter token consumption by early 2026, and Zhipu's own provider page shows GLM-5.2 at 1.29T cumulative tokens.
a 2021 paper (ACL 2022) introduced autoregressive blank infilling with 2D positional encodings, unifying understanding and generation in one objective rather than copying the GPT decoder recipe.
it even raised GLM-5.1 API prices by about 83% to escape the race, risking competitiveness.
